summary: Modal selling their own infra by claiming 3x speedup on a popular image model -- classic vendor benchmark where they control the baseline. 'Pick three' is a marketing line, not an engineering claim.
career_angle: Learn to self-host inference on Modal or similar platforms -- the optimization techniques (batching, quantization, custom kernels) transfer everywhere. But always ask 'faster than what baseline?' before trusting vendor benchmarks.

dimensions.rationale: Modal is a legit YC-backed infra company with real customers, but this is their own marketing blog, not independent analysis.
The snippet is a tagline, not evidence. '3x faster' needs a defined baseline, hardware spec, and methodology to mean anything.
Presumably reproducible on Modal's platform, but vendor-controlled environment makes independent verification harder -- you're testing their claim on their turf.
Direct financial conflict -- Modal profits when you run FLUX on their platform. Every optimization claim is also a sales pitch.
FLUX.1-dev optimizations are real and widely discussed, but '3x' is aggressive. Community benchmarks rarely match vendor claims exactly.
'3x faster' sounds specific but is hollow without stating: faster than what, on which GPU, at what batch size, measuring latency or throughput.
Modal generally delivers on technical claims and has a solid engineering reputation. Not a fly-by-night hype shop.
